cards.title: Last Write Wins (LWW)
cards.body: The most recent update overwrites previous ones. Simple but can lose data. Acceptable for user profiles and settings where the latest value is always correct.

cards.title: Optimistic Locking
cards.body: Each record has a version number. When updating, you send the version you read. If it has changed, the update is rejected and the user must retry. Used by most CRUD applications.

cards.title: Operational Transform (OT)
cards.body: Transforms concurrent operations so they can be applied in any order and produce the same result. Used by Google Docs. Complex to implement correctly.

cards.title: CRDTs
cards.body: Conflict-free Replicated Data Types are data structures that can be merged without conflicts by design. Used by Figma, Linear, and Yjs for real-time collaboration. The most robust solution.
